summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend smoke for GoodGame.center. 34/35 backend tests pass (1 skipped due to onboarding rate limit, not a bug). Frontend home/nav/game-detail/iframe play all OK. Base-href injection verified both at API level and visually in iframe (Neon Starfighter renders & plays).

minor.issue: In-memory rate limit (5/IP/10min) is strict enough to block legitimate same-IP test/preview clients; consider raising for /api/onboarding or scoping by IP+UA in dev. Not a functional bug — limits applied correctly.
Zip with only unsafe members (../, /etc/...) returns 400 'no usable files'. Works as intended; just confirming the error message is generic rather than 'path traversal blocked'.

integration_issues.issue: Could not fully validate post-signup nav swap (upload-game-cta / logout) because backend onboarding rate limit (5/10min) was already exhausted by backend tests from same egress IP. Frontend POSTed correctly; backend returned 429. No functional defect.

issues: Browser warning: 'allow-fullscreen' is an invalid sandbox flag. Should be 'allow-same-origin' / 'allow-scripts' etc. The correct attribute for fullscreen is the iframe's `allow="fullscreen"` (not a sandbox token). Cosmetic console warning only.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Relax /api/onboarding rate-limit (currently 5/IP/10min) or scope to per-IP+UA so legitimate users behind a shared NAT (preview env) aren't blocked.
OPTIONAL: Replace iframe sandbox token 'allow-fullscreen' with `allow="fullscreen"` attribute (separate from sandbox) to silence the console warning.
Verify these are nits — none of them block any feature.
critical_code_review_comments: Rate-limit state is process-local (in-memory dict in auth.py). Loses fairness on multi-worker deploys and resets on backend restart. For production move to Redis/Mongo TTL bucket.
Zip ingest correctly enforces MAX_TOTAL_BYTES, MAX_FILES, compression-ratio (zip-bomb), blocked extensions, and path-traversal — solid.

UGC serve: Sec-Fetch-Dest=document -> 302 to /games/<slug>/play is a great anti-clickjacking pattern; iframe path injects <base href> only into text/html responses and avoids double-injection — correct.
Thumbnail/clip uploads validate content-type and size before persisting — good.
is_real_owner() filter on /api/games + /api/games/{slug} hides legacy/system games — works as intended.
Cookie set with secure=require_secure(request); behind ingress that forwards x-forwarded-proto=https, so cookies are correctly Secure in preview/prod.

success_rate.frontend: 90% (all core flows verified; onboarding-UI end state not visually confirmed due to backend 429)

test_credentials: None seeded. Tests create users on the fly via /api/onboarding. Note: same egress IP shared by backend tests + browser-automation triggers /api/onboarding rate limit (5/10min).
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates ~4-5 ephemeral users (prefix a/b/c/lg/se/lo/ok/dup/ftest), 1-2 test games per session_a, 1 test clip, 1 community. Pre-existing `neon-starfighter` game by `goodgame_tester` left intact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file is at /app/backend/tests/test_goodgame_backend.py. It is pytest-based and uses module-scoped fixtures (session_a, session_b) pre-warmed via _prewarm autouse fixture so the onboarding rate limit (5/IP/10min) is consumed only by 2-3 real onboardings + a few rejected attempts. If you re-run within 10 minutes of UI testing, restart backend (`sudo supervisorctl restart backend`) to reset in-memory rate buckets. The /api/__version, public SPA routes, full auth lifecycle (onboarding/login/session/logout), games CRUD + UGC serving (with Sec-Fetch-Dest behavior + base-href injection assertion), thumbnail/build/patch ownership rules, clip upload + size/type limits, community lifecycle (create/join/post/hide), and reports endpoint are all covered. The /api/ugc/<gid>/index.html iframe response was verified to contain `<base href="/api/ugc/<gid>/">` and a permissive CSP header.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. The single skipped test (TestCommunities.test_full_community_flow) is the third-user onboarding hitting 429 — this is the rate limiter doing its job. The browser onboarding 429 observed in the UI smoke also stems from the same rate limit (backend tests + UI test share the egress IP).
